title: Fix SQL Query to Extract Unique Column Values

statement.markdown:
Write a SQL query to extract unique values from a given column in a table. The query should be able to handle duplicate values and return only unique values.

**Input Format:**
A string representing the SQL query to execute. The query will be in the format `SELECT column_name FROM table_name;`.

**Output Format:**
A string representing the modified SQL query with the unique values extracted.

**Notes:**
- The input query will always be a valid SQL query.
- The table will always have at least one row.
- The column will always exist in the table.

**Examples:**
- **Input:** `SELECT name FROM customers;`
  **Output:** `SELECT DISTINCT name FROM customers;`
- **Input:** `SELECT product FROM orders;`
  **Output:** `SELECT DISTINCT product FROM orders;`
- **Input:** `SELECT country FROM employees;`
  **Output:** `SELECT DISTINCT country FROM employees;`

constraints.description: Number of rows in the table

examples.explanation: The input query has a single column, so the modified query should also have a single column with DISTINCT applied.
The input query has a single column, so the modified query should also have a single column with DISTINCT applied.

hints.text: Notice that the input query already selects a single column. You don't need to modify this part of the query.

editorial.title: Extracting Unique Values
editorial.intuition: To extract unique values from a column, we can use the DISTINCT keyword in the SQL query.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to manually modify the input query by adding the DISTINCT keyword.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use string manipulation to add the DISTINCT keyword to the input query.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a single line of code to add the DISTINCT keyword to the input query.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The modified query should return only unique values from the column.
editorial.dryRun: Try running the modified query on the sample data to see the output.
commonMistakes: forgetting to add the DISTINCT keyword
adding the DISTINCT keyword to the wrong part of the query
